svg.font-family: Segoe UI, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: Private Code Assistant — Deployment View (Layered Architecture)
Qwen3-Coder-30B FP8 · TP=1 · 32k ctx · NVIDIA L40S
All pods serve same model name:
FP8 on NVIDIA · BF16 on Neuron
1. Developer IDE extension calls OpenAI-compatible API
2. Request enters Service Mesh Gateway (Istio + Gateway API)
3. ext_proc filter delegates routing decision to llm-d EPP
4. EPP scores replicas: prefix cache affinity, KV cache headroom, queue
5. Gateway routes request to optimal vLLM pod (any accelerator type)
6. Model inference on GPU / NeuronCores → response streams to IDE
All traffic cluster-internal — no external AI service egress required
EPP + Gateway co-located: EPP advises, Gateway proxies
TLS terminates at each vLLM pod (self-signed certs via cert-manager)
• RHOAI deploys KServe, which provides LLMInferenceService + InferencePool CRDs
• llm-d (Endpoint Picker, Router-Scheduler) is NOT a separate operator—it is CRDs + controllers
• Service Mesh Operator provides Istio + Gateway API integration for intelligent routing
• GPU Operator + Neuron Operator handle device plugin, driver, and monitoring per accelerator
PLATFORM LAYER — RED HAT OPENSHIFT (ROSA HCP 4.21 · K8s 1.34)
Private AI Code Assistant — Deployment View · ROSA HCP 4.21 on AWS · Multi-accelerator heterogeneous inference · 11 operators
